# SPEC: #192 ‚Äî Create meridian:fix-it play ‚Äî RCA-driven defect resolution pipeline
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Create an end-to-end defect resolution play (`meridian:fix-it`) that takes an existing issue, performs thorough root cause analysis, designs a fix with alternatives and risk assessment, presents findings at a single human checkpoint, and upon approval implements the fix and ships it (commit, PR, merge) fully autonomously.

**Constraints:**
- C1: Must require an existing open issue ‚Äî halt if no issue provided or issue not open
- C2: Must not run on main/master/default for implementation ‚Äî branch created if needed
- C3: RCA must trace from symptom to specific root cause (file, line, logic). Restating the issue title is not RCA.
- C4: Fix design must include alternatives considered with reasons for rejection
- C5: Fix design must include risk assessment with severity levels
- C6: Single checkpoint after RCA + design. No checkpoint skipping ‚Äî always presented.
- C7: After checkpoint approval (Tether), all remaining steps execute without human approval
- C8: Implementation reads STM artifacts, never the checkpoint brief (Structure A critical rule)
- C9: Shipping uses the `ship` sub-play with `approval_override: "auto-proceed"`
- C10: Feature branch naming convention: `fix/{issue}-{slug}`

**Failure Conditions:**
- F1: Play proceeds to implementation without human approval at the checkpoint
- F2: RCA output contains no root cause or root cause is identical to the issue title
- F3: Fix design contains no alternatives_considered entries
- F4: Shipping completes partially (commits without PR, or PR without merge)
- F5: Issue does not exist or is not open at play start
- F6: Implementation creates changes in files not listed in the design's affected_files without justification
- F7: Code-builder receives checkpoint brief content in its input contract

### Summary

The `meridian:fix-it` play is a Structure A (full checkpoint flow) high-order play with four phases: (1) Preparation ‚Äî validate issue, create branch, perform RCA with `tech-designer`, design the fix; (2) Checkpoint ‚Äî `doc-builder` generates an HTML brief from RCA/design STM artifacts, human reviews and approves via Tether/Vanish; (3) Execution ‚Äî `code-builder` implements the fix from the approved design, then `ship` sub-play handles commit/PR/merge autonomously; (4) Evidence ‚Äî write delivery record.

The play uses `meridian:` namespace prefix (directory: `fix-it`, frontmatter name: `meridian:fix-it`) following the convention established by `meridian:report-issue`.

### Affected Files

| File | Role | Change Needed |
|------|------|---------------|
| `core/components/plays/fix-it/reference/intent.yaml` | Intent source | Create |
| `core/components/plays/fix-it/SKILL.md` | Compiled play | Create via /create-play |

No existing files need modification. All agents (`tech-designer`, `code-builder`, `doc-builder`, `repo-orchestrator`, `project-orchestrator`) already exist and pass P1-P11 audit.

### Technical Approach

**Strategy:** Structure A (full checkpoint flow) with `ship` sub-play delegation for shipping.

```
Pre-flight ‚Üí [Validate Issue (project-orchestrator)] ‚Üí [Create Branch (repo-orchestrator)]
  ‚Üí [RCA + Design (tech-designer)] ‚Üí [Brief (doc-builder)] ‚Üí CHECKPOINT
  ‚Üí [Implement (code-builder)] ‚Üí [Ship (ship sub-play, auto-proceed)]
  ‚Üí Scenario Validation ‚Üí Evidence
```

**Agents:**

| Agent | Type | Domain | Phase |
|-------|------|--------|-------|
| `project-orchestrator` | domain | Issue validation | Preparation |
| `tech-designer` | domain | RCA, fix design, blast radius | Preparation |
| `code-builder` | domain | Implement fix from execution plan | Execution |
| `doc-builder` | utility | Generate checkpoint HTML brief | Checkpoint |
| `repo-orchestrator` | utility | Branch setup, evidence self-commit | Pre-flight, Evidence |

Domain agent count: 3 (within ‚â§5 budget). Utility agents: 2 (exempt).

**Alternatives Considered:**
- Structure C (pure play chain) ‚Üí rejected: fix-it has novel domain work (RCA, implementation) needing direct agent dispatches
- Invoking commit-code/create-pr/merge-pr individually ‚Üí rejected: `ship` already handles the chain correctly
- Using `implement-epic` for implementation ‚Üí rejected: implement-epic requires locked plan.yaml, features.yaml, and full TDD loop. Fix-it is lighter-weight.
- Two checkpoints (RCA then design) ‚Üí rejected per requirement of single checkpoint

**Risks:**

| Risk | Mitigation | Severity |
|------|-----------|----------|
| Ship sub-play fails mid-way | Status file tracks ship completion; resume picks up from ship's internal status | Medium |
| RCA is superficial | Step eval checks root cause depth: must identify specific code location + why | Medium |
| Implementation diverges from design | Step eval compares implemented files against design's affected_files | Low |
